title: Pharmaceutical Serialization Data Extraction for FDA Compliance
metaTitle: Pharmaceutical Serialization Data Extraction | FDA Compliance
metaDescription: Extract serialization data from pharma packaging documents for FDA compliance and track-and-trace requirements using AI-powered data extraction.
h1: Extract Pharmaceutical Serialization Data for FDA Compliance

hero.headline: Convert Pharmaceutical Packaging Documents to Structured Serialization Data
hero.subheadline: Extract NDC numbers, lot codes, expiration dates, and serial numbers from pharma documents for FDA DSCSA compliance and track-and-trace systems

summary: Pharmaceutical companies need to extract serialization data from packaging documents, labels, and compliance reports to meet FDA Drug Supply Chain Security Act requirements. This involves converting PDF certificates, packaging specifications, and label proofs into structured spreadsheets containing NDC numbers, serial numbers, lot codes, and expiration dates for track-and-trace systems.
whoItsFor: Contract packaging organizations processing drug labels
Supply chain managers tracking serialized products
whenThisIsRelevant: Converting serialization certificates from packaging vendors
Processing batch packaging reports for FDA submissions
Extracting data from label artwork proofs before production
supportedInputs: PDF serialization certificates and compliance reports
Scanned packaging labels with NDC and serial numbers
Digital label artwork files with serialization data
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheets with NDC numbers, lot codes, and serial numbers in separate columns
CSV files formatted for import into track-and-trace systems
commonChallenges: Manual transcription of serial numbers from hundreds of packaging documents
Converting complex packaging specifications into database-ready formats
Extracting variable serialization data from different label layouts
Processing batch reports with mixed document formats
howItWorksSteps: Upload PDF packaging documents or scanned label images
Select specific fields like NDC number, lot code, expiration date, and serial number
AI extracts serialization data using OCR and field recognition
Download structured Excel file with one row per product for compliance tracking
whyPdfExcelAiFits: AI accurately identifies NDC numbers and serial number patterns in packaging documents
Batch processing handles multiple serialization certificates simultaneously
OCR works on both digital PDFs and scanned packaging labels
Custom field selection adapts to different pharmaceutical labeling formats
limitations: Accuracy depends on label print quality and document clarity
Complex multi-product packaging documents may require field customization
Heavily damaged or faded packaging labels may have incomplete data extraction

faq.question: Can this extract NDC numbers from pharmaceutical packaging labels?
faq.answer: Yes, the AI can identify and extract NDC numbers along with other serialization data like lot codes and expiration dates from both digital and scanned pharmaceutical documents.

faq.question: Does this work with different pharmaceutical label formats?
faq.answer: Yes, the system handles various label layouts through custom field selection, though non-standard formats may require some field customization to ensure accurate extraction.

faq.question: How accurate is serialization data extraction from packaging documents?
faq.answer: Accuracy exceeds 99% on clear, high-quality pharmaceutical documents, but depends on label print quality and document clarity for optimal results.

faq.question: Can I process multiple serialization certificates at once?
faq.answer: Yes, batch processing allows you to upload and process multiple pharmaceutical packaging documents simultaneously, with results compiled into a single structured spreadsheet.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
Supports standard pharma document formats including PDF and images
Custom field selection for different serialization requirements
exampleUseCases: Converting packaging vendor certificates into compliance tracking spreadsheets
Extracting serialization data from label proofs before production runs
Processing batch packaging reports for FDA DSCSA submissions
Creating structured databases from pharmaceutical label artwork files
